zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Beauchamp於大約1299年出生在英國薩默塞特哈契時，她的父親John de Beauchamp11297歲，母親Cecily de Vivonne43歲。她於1314年與John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1388年7月1日在英國英格蘭肯特可巴姆享年90歲，埋葬在英國肯特羅徹斯特。

zh-hans.Cobham, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特可巴姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Beauchamp于大约1299年出生在英国薩默塞特哈契时，她的父亲John de Beauchamp11297岁，母亲Cecily de Vivonne43岁。她于1314年与John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1388年7月1日在英国英格兰肯特可巴姆享年90岁，埋葬在英国肯特羅徹斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Beauchamp was born about 1299, in Hatch, Somerset, England, her father, John de Beauchamp, was 11297 and her mother, Cecily de Vivonne, was 43. She married John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham in 1314. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 1 July 1388, in Cobham, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 90, and was buried in Rochester, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Beauchamp est née environ 1299, à Hatch, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John de Beauchamp, avait 11297 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Vivonne, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham en 1314. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 1 juillet 1388, à Cobham, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 90 ans, et a été enterrée à Rochester, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Beauchamp um 1299 in Hatch, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Beauchamp 11297 und ihre Mutter Cecily de Vivonne 43. Sie hat 1314 John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 1. Juli 1388 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Cobham, Kent, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Rochester, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beauchamp è nata il circa 1299 nel Hatch, Somerset, England, sua padre, John de Beauchamp, aveva 11297 anni e sua madre, Cecily de Vivonne, aveva 43 anni. Lei sposato John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham nel 1314. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 1 luglio 1388, nel Cobham, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 90 anni, e fu sepolta a Rochester, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Beauchampが約1299年にイギリスソメルセトハトチで生まれた時，父John de Beauchampは11297歳，母Cecily de Vivonneは43歳であった。1314年にJohn de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1388年7月1日にイギリスイギリスケントコブハムで90歳で死去し，イギリスケントロチェスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1299년 영국 서머세트 해트치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Beauchamp(은)는 11297세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Vivonne(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1314년 John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1388년 7월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 컵함에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 록헤스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1299, em Hatch, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Beauchamp, tinha 11297 anos e sua mãe, Cecily de Vivonne, tinha 43 anos. Ela casou-se com John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham em 1314. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1 de julho de 1388, em Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 90 anos, e foi sepultada em Rochester, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Beauchamp родилась около 1299 г. в местности хач, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Beauchamp было 11297 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Vivonne, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham в 1314 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 1 июля 1388 г. в местности кобхам, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 90 лет и была похоронена в местности рошестир, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1299, en Hatch, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Beauchamp, tenía 11297 años y su mamá, Cecily de Vivonne, tenía 43 años. Se casó con John de Cobham, 2nd Baron Cobham en 1314. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 1 de julio de 1388, en Cobham, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 90 años, y fue sepultada en Rochester, Kent, Inglaterra.
